message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~2,369 reviews (Palm Desert location); Tripadvisor ~1,672 reviews; combined with Palm Springs sibling location pushes the brand to 6,000+ reviews across platforms
- Sentiment: mostly positive with pricing-driven mixed undercurrent
- Longevity: Established 1963 by Sherman Harris; 60+ years operating; still family-owned and run by Harris's children Sam and Janet — ownership stability confirmed across multiple sources
- Credentials: ["Featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives with Guy Fieri (Palm Desert location)","KESQ Best of Coachella Valley — Best Desserts winner","Palm Springs Life feature ('Family Ties')","Listed among Visit Palm Springs' '3 longest-operating restaurants'","Palm Desert Area Chamber of Commerce member"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best Jewish deli Palm Springs/Palm Desert/Coachella Valley', 'Diners Drive-Ins and Dives Palm Springs', 'pastrami sandwich Coachella Valley', and 'longest-operating restaurants Palm Springs'. Does NOT surface in generic 'best breakfast Palm Desert' lists (Wilma & Frieda's, Paradise Cove, Keedy's dominate) despite serving full breakfast daily — a notable gap given their cinnamon-bread French toast and 8-egg omelets. Does not appear in 'best bakery Palm Desert' agent answers despite baking up to 100 loaves of rye daily.
- Site structured data: shermansdeli.com has clean location/hours/phone content and a functional menu structure, but NO JSON-LD or schema.org microdata detected. No Restaurant, LocalBusiness, Menu, or PostalAddress schema. Hours and addresses are human-readable only. Online ordering routes through NetWaiter (third-party). This is a meaningful agent-readability gap for a 60-year landmark brand.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
# Sherman's Deli & Bakery - Structured Data Analysis

## Findings:

**(1) JSON-LD script tags:** No `application/ld+json` script tags are present in the provided HTML source.

**(2) Schema.org microdata:** No `itemtype` or `itemscope` attributes found in the HTML.

**(3) Hours, address, phone as plain text:** Yes. Contact details appear only as unstructured text:
- "We're open daily 8am to 9pm"
- Palm Springs: "401 E. Tahquitz Canyon Way" and "760-325-1199"
- Palm Desert: "73161 Country Club Drive" and "760-568-1350"

**(4) Restaurant/LocalBusiness schemas:** None detected. The page contains no formal structured data markup for Restaurant, LocalBusiness, Menu, or PostalAddress schemas.

**Summary:** This website relies entirely on human-readable text formatting for business information without implementing any machine-readable structured data markup. Search engines and assistants cannot automatically parse the business details through schema.org or JSON-LD formats.

Based on the search results, here's the history of Sherman's Deli in Palm Desert:

Sherman Harris launched his eponymous restaurant in 1963 when Palm Springs was the playground for Hollywood icons. Sherman Harris was born on October 24, 1923 in Superior, Wisconsin. He joined the army at the age of 18 and fought in 5 major battles, including the Battle of the Bulge. Sherman returned home in 1946 on the Queen Mary with a German shepherd puppy, rescued and hidden in his duffel bag. Sherman and his family moved to Palm Springs, California in 1953.

His friend Al Laury needed help in his restaurant, Laury's Steak Ranch. Sherman worked as a cook at Laury's and there originated "early bird dinners" in 1954. Twenty five years later, Sherman had 5 restaurants/delicatessens of his own in the Palm Springs area. His first deli was located in Palm Springs across from the Spa Hotel.

A New York-style family restaurant still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet. With two great locations – Palm Springs and Palm Desert – it's easy to make Sherman's your favorite place!

Links: [{"title":"Recap: Diner's, Drive-Ins and Dives at Sherman's Deli in Palm Desert","url":"https://cactushugs.com/shermans-deli-palm-springs-diners-drive-ins-dives/"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Springs, CA) on Diners, Drive-Ins & Dives","url":"https://www.dinersdriveinsdiveslocations.com/shermans-deli-bakery-california.html"},{"title":"Guy Fieri’s Tasty Desert Stops | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/guy-fieri/"},{"title":"Guy Fieri Discovers The BEST Deli In California (Shermans Deli) | Diners, Drive-Ins & Dives - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CObxZ5lzomY"},{"title":"Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Featured on TV Show: Sherman's Deli - Review of Sherman's Deli, Palm Desert, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32846-d535463-r482980155-Sherman_s_Deli-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives in Palm Springs: Every Guy Fieri Stop | Cactus Hugs","url":"https://cactushugs.com/diners-drive-ins-dives-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery - Palm Springs & Palm Desert, California - Gil's Thrilling (And Filling) Blog","url":"https://www.nmgastronome.com/?p=44840"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery","url":"https://shermansdeli.com/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sherman's Deli in Palm Desert and its appearance on Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives:

## Sherman's Deli on Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

Guy Fieri visited Sherman's Deli and Bakery in Palm Desert for his Food Network show. The episode first aired on May 12, 2017, titled "Turkey, Taters and Dogs."

### Featured Dishes

Sherman's delivered full deli nostalgia for Guy Fieri, including turkey pastrami, house-made rye bread, and a latke brisket sandwich that leaned hard into classic Jewish deli tradition. Guy headed to the kitchen with chef Gordon Poster to bake Sherman's rye bread, which they prepare in-house and make up to 100 loaves a day.

### Impact of the Appearance

The show had a significant effect on the restaurant. After the show aired, the turkey pastrami became the most popular sandwich on the menu, catapulting the number of turkey breasts prepared each week from 10 to 30.

### About Sherman's

Sherman's opened its first location in 1963 and has become a landmark in the desert, still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son Sam and daughter Janet. The restaurant has two locations in Palm Springs and Palm Desert.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the best breakfast options in Palm Desert, including the restaurants you mentioned:

## Wilma & Frieda's Cafe

This cafe is named after the owner's two grandmothers, Wilma and Frieda, and it's highly regarded for breakfast. Wilma and Frieda's has won "Best Breakfast" from Palm Springs Life three times and won 2021 Best of the Desert from the Desert Sun. 

Wilma & Frieda's Café is an El Paseo Shopping District mandatory stop for crispy, shredded hashbrowns with your egg breakfast, with classic comfort foods like waffles and brunchy choices updated with fresh ingredients. You must try the homemade biscuits and strawberry jam.

## Keedy's Fountain & Grill

This spot has been dishing out all-day breakfasts and heartwarming meals since 1957. Keedy's Fountain & Grill is a genuine vintage diner with counter seating and is a breakfast joint for reading the paper over endless coffee and a stack of pancakes.

Keedy's serves the "World's Best Pancakes" in their authentic throwback diner with a 50's feel. The Corned Beef Hash deserves a standing ovation.

Both restaurants are consistently recommended as top breakfast destinations in Palm Desert!

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Jewish delis in the Coachella Valley (Palm Springs and Palm Desert area):

## Top Recommendations:

**Sherman's Deli & Bakery**
Described as "Coachella Valley's favorite Jewish deli and bakery," Sherman's has two locations—one in Palm Springs and one in Palm Desert. Established in 1963, Sherman's has become a landmark in the desert. It's a New York-style family restaurant that offers an extensive menu for breakfast, brunch, lunch, & dinner reminiscent of a New York style deli.

**Manhattan in the Desert**
Manhattan in the Desert is a genuine New York-style Jewish deli specializing in lox, homemade soups, hot dogs and sandwiches, the latter of which are all served "mile-high," stacked to perfection and with a free sampling of delicious pickled goods. The deli also boasts a sister location in Palm Desert (which was featured on "Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives!"), but the original is the best.

**Other Notable Options:**

- Deli Chick has the best sandwiches in the Coachella area with a clean, casual and friendly atmosphere.
- J's Deli is located in Palm Desert around the corner from El Paseo, this new Deli is large and clean with a variety of dishes for all times of the year.

Based on the search results for bakeries in Palm Desert with rye bread, here are the top options:

## Best Bakeries in Palm Desert for Rye Bread

The top-rated bakeries in Palm Desert include A Bakery, Family Bakery, La Provence Patisserie, Sherman's Deli & Bakery, and Aspen Mills Bakery.

**Sherman's Deli & Bakery** is particularly notable as it has an on-site bakery and makes its bread in-house daily. Additionally, they offer a variety of breads, including French baguettes, sourdough, and rye, among others.

**Aspen Mills Bakery** is also worth considering, as it's easily the best bakery in the area with breads and pastries that are absolutely excellent.

Both of these bakeries are consistently mentioned among the best options in the Palm Desert area and offer quality rye bread along with a variety of other fresh-baked products.

I've found information about Sherman's Deli & Bakery and the longest-operating restaurants in Palm Springs. Here's what the search revealed:

## Sherman's Deli & Bakery

Sherman's Deli & Bakery was established in 1963 by Sherman Harris, a New York native who brought the flavors of a traditional Jewish deli to the desert. The restaurant is still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet, with locations in Palm Springs and Palm Desert.

Sherman's is known for hearty deli sandwiches, with specialties including thick slices of tender, juicy pastrami piled high on rye bread, with pastrami brined, seasoned, and smoked in-house.

## The Visit Palm Springs Resource

The Visit Palm Springs website features an article on the longest-operating restaurants in Palm Springs that highlights Sherman's along with other historic establishments like Las Casuelas and The Purple Room. Sherman's stands out as one of the enduring culinary landmarks in the area, maintaining the original quality and values since its founding over 60 years ago.

content.text: I have enough verification. Let me finalize the assessment.

confirms: Yelp Palm Desert review count (~2,369) matches search result snippets showing '1835 Photos & 2369 Reviews'.
Tripadvisor Palm Desert ~1,672 reviews confirmed via search result.
Established 1963 by Sherman Harris; still owned/operated by his children Sam and Janet — confirmed across shermansdeli.com, Palm Springs Life 'Family Ties' feature, Wikipedia, and Visit Palm Springs.
Featured on Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives with Guy Fieri — confirmed (episode 'Turkey, Taters and Dogs,' aired May 12, 2017); the DDD location was Palm Desert (per Cactus Hugs and Tripadvisor).
Palm Springs Life 'Family Ties' feature exists and is live.
Visit Palm Springs '3 longest-operating restaurants' listicle exists and includes Sherman's.
Surfaces reliably for 'best Jewish deli Palm Springs/Palm Desert/Coachella Valley' — confirmed; it is the top-cited answer alongside Manhattan in the Desert.

Site structured-data gap confirmed: shermansdeli.com has NO JSON-LD application/ld+json scripts, NO schema.org microdata (no itemscope/itemtype), and no Restaurant/LocalBusiness/Menu/PostalAddress schema. Hours/address/phone are plain text only.
Bakes up to ~100 loaves of rye daily — confirmed in DDD coverage.
Does NOT dominate 'best breakfast Palm Desert' lists — confirmed; Wilma & Frieda's and Keedy's lead those rankings.
KESQ Best of Coachella Valley — Best Desserts winner confirmed via search result snippet.
contradicts: Claim that DDD featured the 'Palm Desert location' is partially muddled in public sources — multiple references (Cactus Hugs, dinersdriveinsdiveslocations.com) tag the DDD stop as Palm Springs, while Tripadvisor's review page and other sources tag Palm Desert. The Palm Desert attribution is defensible but not unambiguous; treat as 'Palm Desert per Tripadvisor/Palm Springs Life, Palm Springs per some episode trackers.'

Claim that Sherman's does NOT appear in 'best bakery Palm Desert' agent answers is too strong — Sherman's Deli & Bakery IS surfaced by agent-style summaries of Palm Desert bakeries (alongside Aspen Mills, A Bakery, La Provence). It is not the top-of-mind answer for 'bakery,' but it does appear.
flagged_as_unverified: Combined '6,000+ reviews across platforms' aggregate — Yelp PD 2,369 + Yelp PS 4,127 + Tripadvisor PD 1,672 already exceeds 8,000, so the floor is conservative, but I did not independently confirm Tripadvisor PS count or Google review count to validate the specific aggregation methodology.
Sentiment characterization ('mostly positive with pricing-driven mixed undercurrent') — plausible and consistent with deli-segment norms but not independently quantified in this pass.
Palm Desert Area Chamber of Commerce membership — not surfaced in search; could not confirm or refute.
Online ordering routes through NetWaiter — not verified in this pass (would require deeper site crawl beyond homepage).

Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier — subjective scoring; not independently testable, but the underlying signals (zero JSON-LD, strong category dominance, weak breakfast crossover) are consistent with a mid-range gap.
input.notes: The core assessment holds. Longevity, ownership, DDD feature, Palm Springs Life feature, Visit Palm Springs inclusion, and the structured-data gap are all independently confirmed. The most important refutable nuance: the 'does not appear in best bakery Palm Desert agent answers' claim is too absolute — Sherman's does surface in bakery-context agent answers, just not as the leader. Also: which DDD location was featured (Palm Springs vs Palm Desert) is muddled across sources; the brief should soften to 'featured on DDD' without over-specifying the location, or cite both attributions.
